transcript: Speaker A: Uh, I have many work today and my brain... Speaker B: Okay, no worries. Take your time. Speaker A: Yes, my husband filmed me without telling me. Yes, I cried because of my English. But it showed me how badly I needed to improve my English speaking skills. So the next day, practicing with Loora. Let me show you. Speaker C: What are you doing? Speaker A: I am working. I have many work today. Speaker C: Do you mean: 'I have a lot of work today'? Speaker A: Yes, I have a lot of work today and my brain not working. Speaker C: Do you mean: 'my brain isn't working'? Speaker A: Yes, I have a lot of work and my brain isn't working! Speaker C: That's perfect! Well done, Anna! Speaker A: I want to speak in English everyday but lesson is expensive! Speaker C: Why don't you speak to an AI tutor? Speaker A: I'm not sure about an AI tutor. Speaker C: Download the Loora app and talk to me everyday and I'll help correct your mistakes! Speaker A: Amazing. I'll add it to my daily routine today!

summary: A woman struggles with English on a work call, prompting her to find a solution. She demonstrates using the Loora app, where an AI tutor corrects her grammar in real-time. The ad concludes with a call to download the app for daily practice.
